content:
# 2026-05-26

- [ ] Review [[Auth v2]] cutover plan
- [ ] Send weekly status to leads
- Met with @helena re: latency spike on auth.refresh — tracked under [[Auth v2]].

# 2026-05-25

- Finished draft of [[Billing gRPC migration]] RFC
- Quick note: capacity in eu-west-2 is blocking [[Multi-region failover]].

# 2026-05-24

- Reviewed [[SRE checklist]] before on-call rotation hands off.
- Idea: cache warm-up step before failover dry-run.

# Auth v2

Migrate session storage to Redis, cookie-based refresh tokens.

## Status
- Shim is dual-writing.
- Holding rollout until p95 < 250ms.

## Open items
- [ ] Confirm cookie issuer gating
- [ ] Postmortem template prepared

## Refs
- [[SRE checklist]]

# Billing gRPC migration

Move the billing-api REST surface to gRPC.

## Plan
1. Generate proto from existing OpenAPI
2. Dual-serve REST+gRPC for two weeks
3. Migrate internal callers
4. Remove REST surface in v2.0

## Risks
- Java client lacks robust gRPC retry config — see [[SRE checklist]] section on retries.

# Multi-region failover

Goal: fail traffic from us-east-1 to eu-west-2 within 90s.

## Blockers
- eu-west-2 capacity request pending.
- Need cross-region replication validation for billing DB.

# SRE checklist

## Pre-deploy
- [ ] Capacity headroom > 30%
- [ ] Rollback script tested

## Retries
- Use exponential backoff with jitter, cap at 5 retries.
- For gRPC, only retry on UNAVAILABLE / DEADLINE_EXCEEDED.

## Post-incident
- Postmortem template in /templates.

# Postgres tuning notes

- `work_mem` per-connection × max_connections — beware of OOM on bursty workloads.
- `shared_buffers`: 25% of RAM as a starting point.
- Vacuum analyze after bulk loads.
- idea: pre-warm L7 caches before failover\n- TODO: ask infra about eu-west-2 capacity request\n
